verdict_reasoning: JAK1 is a soluble cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase of the Jak family. Although it associates with the intracellular tails of activated cytokine receptors via its FERM domain, it lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any membrane-anchoring motif. JAK1 is recruited to the inner leaflet / cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane transiently during signal transduction but does not reach the outer face or possess a stable membrane anchor of its own. The term 'membrane protein' in the summary refers to its role in membrane-proximal signaling complexes, not to its localization on the cell surface.
